steps.title: Find the explicit point of disagreement
steps.content: Text 1 says the moon formed from debris "over the course of thousands of years." Text 2 says formation "happened immediately after impact, taking just a few hours." The contrast is entirely about speed of formation.

steps.title: Match that contrast to a choice
steps.content: The correct answer should describe Text 1 as viewing formation as slower and Text 2 as viewing it as faster.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "The author of Text 1 argues that the formation of the moon occurred much earlier than the author of Text 2 argues.": overreach — neither text dates when the collision happened; they disagree on duration, not date. ✗
  • "The author of Text 1 suggests there is more evidence confirming the existence of Theia than the author of Text 2 suggests.": Theia's existence is not disputed in either text. ✗
  • "The author of Text 1 claims that the moon's surface is more similar to Earth's surface than the author of Text 2 claims.": neither text discusses surface composition or similarity. ✗
  • "The author of Text 1 believes that the moon formed more slowly than the author of Text 2 believes.": directly captures the thousands-of-years vs. hours contrast. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
